metadata.source: Existing Japanese package + train/validation statistics + uploaded Japanese LN papers
metadata.design_summary: Use conservative direct MFR only for safe recurrent mappings; send boundary-sensitive, pronunciation-variation, punctuation-insertion, and named-entity expansions to target-span prompt/model fallback.
paper_basis: Kondo et al. 2025: Japanese SNS text normalization taxonomy with 6 categories and 33 subcategories; missing symbols, casual/formal endings, Hira/Kata/Kanji variants, and abbreviations were especially useful, while pronunciation variations may encode emotion.

Higashiyama et al. 2021: Japanese UGT corpus for morphological analysis and lexical normalization; categories include neologisms/slang, proper names, onomatopoeia, interjections, dialect words, foreign words, emoticons/AA, character type variants, sound change variants, alternative representations, and typos.

Higashiyama & Utiyama 2025: JMLN frames Japanese LN as boundary-aware span extraction and conversion for unsegmented languages; compact encoders are high-precision while decoder-only models improve recall.

top_tp.norm: 土曜日 と 日曜日

stats.v2_note: Stats are inherited from the previously generated Japanese data analysis; validation split contains Japanese rows and direct high-confidence MFR had low recall but zero FP in prior diagnosis.
